transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks for calling Bright Path Care Navigation, this is Ava. What's going on today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Hi, um, I've had a really bad headache since yesterday and now my vision's a little blurry.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay, I want to make sure you're safe. Any numbness, trouble speaking, or weakness on one side of your body?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: No, nothing like that. Just the headache and blurry vision, and I feel a bit nauseous.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it. Have you taken anything for it, and do you have a history of migraines?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: I took some ibuprofen a few hours ago, didn't help much. I do get migraines sometimes, but this feels worse.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Given the blurry vision is new and different from your usual migraines, I'd recommend being seen today rather than waiting. This sounds like it needs urgent care evaluation, not emergency room, since you have no stroke-type symptoms.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Okay, that makes sense. Can you help me find a place nearby?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Sure, there's QuickCare Urgent Clinic on Maple Avenue, open until 9pm tonight. Want me to send you the address and check current wait times?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Yes please, that would be great.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I've texted you the address and their wait time, about 20 minutes right now. If the vision changes get worse or you develop weakness, please go straight to the ER.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Understood, thank you so much for your help.
